search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae irritable bowel syndrome guideline UAE
site:nice.org.uk/guidance/cg61 IBS adults recommendations symptoms tests follow-up
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Commercial diet pages were not accepted.
Medicine names and doses were not mapped.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for adults meeting symptom-duration and diagnostic criteria; the legacy workflow has no age or 6-month duration restriction.
setting_applicability: Direct for adult primary-care symptom assessment, selected tests, lifestyle discussion, and follow-up.
UAE_applicability: International NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review and local investigation, dietetic, and referral pathways.
recency_verification: The current official CG61 recommendations and 2025 management-review status were checked on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: CG61 remains the current official NICE IBS guideline.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped adult IBS symptom terms, abdominal pain, bloating, bowel-pattern, stool-pattern, food or stress context, urgency, straining, presets, and structured history with diagnostic qualifiers preserved.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports at least 6 months of abdominal pain or discomfort, bloating, bowel-habit change, relation to defaecation or stool frequency or form, straining, urgency, incomplete evacuation, food association, mucus, and selected extra-intestinal symptoms.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Was reviewed for the requirement to assess cancer and inflammatory-bowel-disease features; no specific legacy alarm item is mapped without its own exact source.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires clinical assessment and examination for suspected-cancer features and inflammatory markers for inflammatory bowel disease, with referral when present; specific cancer-feature fields require their own exact source.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed blood, CBC, CRP, and coeliac-serology fields in people meeting IBS criteria.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports full blood count, ESR or plasma viscosity, CRP, and coeliac antibodies in people meeting IBS criteria and identifies tests not required solely to confirm IBS.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-discussed lifestyle and dietary documentation without inserting specific advice automatically.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented lifestyle, activity, diet, symptom-trigger, and specialist dietary discussion; specific advice is not inserted automatically.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped agreed follow-up, symptom-change review, safety, and clinician referral fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports agreed symptom-response-based follow-up, annual review, and investigation or referral when new red-flag symptoms emerge.

unresolved_source_gaps: People younger than 18 years and people not meeting symptom-duration and diagnostic criteria are outside the exact source.
Bloating and abdominal-cramping aliases are too broad for exact matching support.

Specific rectal bleeding, weight loss, nocturnal symptoms, family history, age-at-onset, anaemia, examination fields, stool studies, previous records, generic clinician plan, medication plan, fixed follow-up ranges, and PRN wording remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, red-flag interpretation, treatment, medicine dosing, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
